title: microlicia variolosa
intro: Microlicia variolosa, also known as the Brazilian senna or small leaf senna, is a plant species that belongs to the Fabaceae family. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a laxative and reduce fever. The health benefits of Microlicia variolosa include its use in treating constipation, diarrhea, and various skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in combination with other herbs to create teas, infusions, and capsules, and is traditionally used by indigenous communities in Brazil for its medicinal and spiritual properties.
